evidence_quotes.quote: This kind of behaviour would fit perfectly under the stated practice "unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns"

paragraphs.text: Esteemed members, as a proud european citizen I always realized that many freedoms that I and many others have been able to utilize found either their origin or support from initiatives such as this. One massive aspect EU citizens had the privilige of employing is the consumer protection initiatives that are unrivaled in the world. And the Digital Fairness Act is no different in this endeavor. With that being said, I would like to provide feedbak to add an additional aspect under this umbrella. This concerns the state of digital entertainment content. Specifically digital video games. A medium that combines artistry in visual, musical and writing, engineering, innovation and interactivity. No other entertainment medium can compare to the passion and dedication it has brought to the entire world. Now, this very medium is under serious danger and has been for many years. The preservation of video games is arguably as important and saving paintings, sculptures and scriptures for future generations. These days it is common practice of the largest game publishers to not only make games completely impossible to obtain via commerce, but also to rip purchased products from people that payed for this product (and potentially more via ingame transactions). These actors have used a "loophole" in the current law to preserve the right to withdraw any purchased game from people's ownership. As an example, imagine you buy a new phone. You use it every day and buy and install apps. Then one day you check your pocket and it's gone. You contact the manufacturer and they tell you and new version of this phone is now available so everyone that bought this old phone had their ownership revoked. No reimbursement for neither the phone nor the purchased apps. This is what every big video game publisher has the power to do. This kind of behaviour would fit perfectly under the stated practice "unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns". I hope this explanation made it clear on how much consumers are being exploited to this day. Thank you for your service! ad populum
